questions.question_text: When must a South Dakota broker associate obtain Errors and Omissions insurance?
distractors: Only after closing their first transaction
questions.explanation: E&O insurance is a prerequisite for licensure in South Dakota under SDCL 36-21A-119. The policy must be in place BEFORE the license is issued, not after. This protects consumers from the very first transaction.

questions.question_text: What are the current E&O insurance coverage limits in South Dakota?
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires $100,000 per claim and $500,000 aggregate coverage for 2025. The deductible is $1,000 for damages and $500 for expenses. The SDREC offers a group policy program to help licensees obtain affordable coverage.

questions.question_text: A broker associate allows their E&O policy to lapse without obtaining new coverage. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: Grounds for disciplinary action by the SDREC
distractors: A $50 late fee until coverage is restored
No consequence if they are not actively selling
questions.explanation: Failure to maintain E&O coverage is grounds for disciplinary action. E&O insurance is mandatory for ALL active brokers, broker associates, property managers, auctioneers, and residential rental agents in South Dakota.

questions.question_text: Which type of SD licensee is NOT required to carry E&O insurance?
questions.correct_answer: All active licensees must carry E&O insurance
distractors: Property managers with fewer than 10 units
Broker associates in their first year
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires E&O insurance for ALL active licensees without exception — brokers, broker associates, property managers, auctioneers, and residential rental agents. The SDREC group policy program makes coverage accessible to all license types.

questions.question_text: The SDREC helps licensees obtain E&O coverage through what mechanism?
distractors: Mandatory coverage through the Recovery Fund
questions.explanation: The SDREC offers a group policy program that allows licensees to obtain E&O coverage at competitive rates. This is a practical benefit — licensees can purchase through the group plan or obtain their own individual policy meeting the minimum requirements.
